title: Encode and Decode Strings
prompt: Design an algorithm to encode a list of strings to a single string and decode that single string back into the original list. Strings can contain any unicode characters including separators or digits.
constraints: strs[i] may contain any printable characters.
hints: Any sentinel character could appear in the data — naive splitting fails.
Length-prefix each string: write the length, a delimiter, then the string.
On decode, read up to the delimiter to get the length, then take exactly that many chars.

optimal.approach: Length-prefix each string with `len#str`; decode reads length, then exactly len characters.

alternatives.note: Possible but easy to get wrong with Unicode.

pitfalls: Choosing a delimiter that may appear in data.
